title: The effects of state paid sick leave mandates on parental childcare time

construct_vars: gvar_tbl <- df %>% dplyr::filter(pslm_state_lag2 == 1) %>% dplyr::group_by(fips) %>% dplyr::summarise(first_t = min(time), .groups='drop')
df <- df %>% dplyr::left_join(gvar_tbl, by='fips') %>% dplyr::mutate(gvar_CS = ifelse(!is.na(first_t), as.numeric(first_t), 0))

notes: EVENT STUDY REVIEWED. Maclean & Pabilonia (2025) JHE. IMPORTANT: Paper's MAIN estimator is Gardner (did2s), NOT TWFE. TWFE is reported only in Appendix Table 10 as one of 5 alternative estimators (Gardner, BJS, Wooldridge, Stacked DiD, TWFE). Appendix Table 10 TWFE: reghdfe carehh_k pslm_state_lag2 controls [pw=wt06], a(fips time) cl(fips). RCS data (139K obs, 78K with hh_child==1). Monthly time (239 periods), 44 states, 10 treated states (6 cohorts: 2014,2017,2018,2019,2020,2022). Paper uses 'carehh_k' = primary childcare minutes per average day. SIGN REVERSAL: TWFE=+4.609** vs CS-NT<0 (ns). Bacon decomposition shows cohort ~2020 (ATT=+10.12, weight=34%) drives positive TWFE — confounded with COVID lockdowns (PSL lagged 2yr → 2018 adoption activates 2020). Cohorts 2017 and 2022 show negative ATTs. GARDNER DIAGNOSTIC: Gardner agrees with TWFE (both positive early post-treatment, negative at late horizons). Cohort-specific Gardner: wildly heterogeneous. TWFE/Gardner agreement explained by shared cohort-size weighting (Cohort 2017=40%, 2020=25% dominate), while CS uses uniform group×time weighting. Conclusion: sign reversal is real — driven by COVID-confounded Cohort 2020 dominating size-weighted estimators (TWFE, Gardner) while CS distributes weight more evenly. | CS-DID IMPLEMENTATION NOTE (2026-04-19): CS-DID runs directly on the 78k monthly individual-level RCS data via att_gt(panel=FALSE). Earlier template used a state×year pre-aggregation step (removed 2026-04-19) — that step dropped the individual-level covariates from the CS-DID regression, blocking Spec A. Running CS-DID directly on the full RCS data: (a) takes ~2 minutes but runs cleanly, (b) preserves all individual covariates for Spec A, (c) gives a larger-magnitude CS-NT estimate (sign still reverses vs TWFE) because the ATT(g,t) cells are finer with 239 months vs 20 years.
